protocol.name: aios_compositor_v1
protocol.copyright: Copyright © 2026 AIOS Product Team Permission to use, copy, modify, and distribute this software is licensed under the MIT License.

interface.name: aios_compositor_manager_v1
interface.description: This interface allows a privileged client (the AI System Daemon) to monitor and control windows, capture frames, and inject input events into the AI session. Only clients running as root (UID 0) are permitted to bind to this interface.

request.name: get_session
request.description: Request control over a specific desktop session. session_type 0 = Human session (read-only monitoring allowed), session_type 1 = AI session (full write/input control allowed).

interface.name: aios_session_v1
interface.description: Interface for inspecting and controlling windows and inputs within a session.

request.name: get_windows
request.description: Requests the compositor to send info for all mapped windows in this session.

event.name: window_info
event.description: Sent in response to get_windows for each window.

event.name: window_list_done
event.description: Sent after all window_info events have been dispatched.

request.name: capture_screen

description.summary: request a screenshot of the session

request.description: Triggers a screenshot capture. The compositor will send a capture_buffer event containing the frame dimensions and a shared memory file descriptor.

event.name: capture_buffer
event.description: Provides screen buffer data via a shared memory file descriptor (shm).

event.name: capture_failed
event.description: Sent if a screenshot capture request could not be completed.

request.name: inject_key

description.summary: simulate a keyboard key press or release

request.description: Injects a keyboard event into this session's seat input pipeline.

request.name: inject_pointer_motion

description.summary: simulate relative or absolute pointer movement

request.description: Moves the pointer inside this session. If absolute is non-zero, moves to coordinates (x, y); otherwise, moves relatively by dx, dy.
